title: betula papyrifera var. kenaica
intro: Betula papyrifera var. kenaica, a subspecies of the paper birch tree, is a deciduous tree native to the western coast of North America. The bark of this tree contains salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which is commonly used in herbal medicine. The medicinal properties of Betula papyrifera var. kenaica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of this plant include tinctures, teas, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from respiratory problems to wound healing.
